description: Project scaffolding — monorepo with ts-executor/ and py-engine/ services, shared/schemas/ for message contracts, Docker Compose config, and local dev tooling
steps: ts-executor/ builds and runs with TypeScript strict mode, no errors
py-engine/ runs with no import errors; dependencies locked in pyproject.toml
shared/schemas/ contains JSON schemas that both services validate against
docker-compose.yml brings up Redis + PostgreSQL + both services
README with setup instructions
.env.example documents all required environment variables

description: Redis Streams communication — three channels (market:events, execution:orders, execution:results) using Redis Streams with MAXLEN pruning, plus cache layer for prices, gas, and pool states
steps: All three channels use Redis Streams (not pub/sub) with consumer group support
MAXLEN pruning on every write (STREAM_MAX_LENGTH env var, default 10000) plus periodic trim in decision loop
Cache layer operational for prices, gas, and pool states with configurable TTLs
Both services validate all messages against shared JSON schemas at the boundary
Schema violations are rejected with structured error logs, not silently consumed
Connection loss events surfaced via callbacks with reconnect backoff

description: JSON schema contracts — shared message format definitions for market:events, execution:orders, and execution:results Redis channels
steps: market-events.schema.json defines all event types (new_block, swap, rate_change, liquidity_change, large_transfer, price_update)
execution-orders.schema.json defines order format with chain/protocol enums, action types, params, limits
execution-results.schema.json defines TX result format with status, hash, gas used
Both services load and validate against the same schema files
Chain enum: [ethereum, base]. Protocol enum: [aave_v3, aerodrome]

description: Docker Compose local development — Redis + PostgreSQL + both services with hot-reload dev targets
steps: docker-compose.yml brings up Redis, PostgreSQL, ts-executor, py-engine
Dev targets: tsx watch for TS, watchfiles auto-reload for Python
Volume mounts for live code editing
Environment injection via .env file
Container networking via redis/postgres hostnames

description: PostgreSQL database — persistent storage for trade history, portfolio state, strategy statuses, and decision audit log
steps: Database models for portfolio positions, trade history, strategy statuses, decision audit log
Repository layer for CRUD operations
SQLAlchemy create_all() for schema initialization
DATABASE_URL environment variable configuration
In-memory cache loaded at startup, kept current from execution:results

description: Main decision loop — wires all modules in both services. py-engine: enrich → strategy eval → decision gate → Claude API → verify → emit. ts-executor: component init, order subscription, graceful shutdown
steps: Python DecisionLoop class orchestrates the full pipeline
Decision gate: opens when any strategy report has actionable: true signal, no API call otherwise
All strategy reports included in prompt when gate opens (even from earlier cycles)
TypeScript main() initializes WS manager, publisher, L2 listener, TX builder, Safe wallet
Graceful shutdown with signal handling in both services
Periodic stream trimming (STREAM_TRIM_INTERVAL_CYCLES env var)

description: Alchemy WebSocket subscription manager — persistent WebSocket connections via viem with reconnection, backpressure, and health monitoring
steps: WebSocket connection via viem with custom reconnection logic
Exponential backoff reconnection: 200ms initial, 2x growth, 30s cap, resets on success
Health monitoring: configurable timeout (default 60s), alerts on prolonged silence
Rate limit detection (429 errors) with backpressure queue (max 1000 events)
Block watcher and contract event subscriptions that survive reconnects
Monotonically increasing sequence numbers for event ordering

description: Market event publisher — transforms raw blockchain events into structured, schema-validated market:events and publishes to Redis Streams
steps: Event normalizer supports all 6 market-events schema types
Normalizes raw chain events to schema-compliant format
Publishes to market:events Redis Stream with schema validation
Drops invalid events with structured error logging

description: Base L2 listener — chain event listening for Base with L2-specific gas estimation (OP Stack model + overhead)
steps: Base chain event subscription via Alchemy WebSocket
L2 gas estimation: OP Stack model with data posting overhead
Events normalized to market-events schema with chain: base
Configurable via environment variables (RPC URL, contract addresses)
Finality configuration: configurable finalityBlocks (default 12) for Base L2

description: Transaction builder — consumes orders from execution:orders, constructs and sends transactions via viem through Safe wallet, with retry logic and result publishing
steps: Subscribes to execution:orders Redis Stream
Routes orders to protocol-specific encode modules via adapter map
Pre-flight checks: deadline expiry rejection, gas ceiling enforcement
Executes via SafeWalletManager (validateOrder → execute → recordSpend)
Retry logic: exponential backoff, max 3 retries, non-retryable error detection
Publishes results to execution:results (schema-validated)
All operations logged with correlation IDs
Finality-aware confirmation: configurable timeout, waits for sufficient block confirmations

description: Safe 1-of-2 multisig wallet — battle-tested wallet with agent EOA + human recovery signer, application-level contract allowlist and spending limits
steps: 1-of-2 Safe: agent EOA owner #1, human recovery owner #2, threshold=1
Application-level contract allowlist: case-insensitive, rejects non-allowlisted targets
Spending limits: per-TX cap + daily cap with UTC midnight reset
Combined validateOrder() runs both allowlist + spending checks
Balance queries: ETH and ERC-20 via Alchemy
Transaction execution via Safe{Core} SDK
Private key never appears in logs or error messages

description: Aave V3 encode module — encode-only pure functions for supply and withdraw calldata on Base
steps: encodeSupply(): produces ABI-encoded calldata for Aave V3 Pool.supply()
encodeWithdraw(): produces ABI-encoded calldata for Aave V3 Pool.withdraw()
Pure functions — no state, no side effects, no chain calls
Wired into buildAdapterMap() in index.ts

description: Aerodrome encode module — encode-only pure functions for Aerodrome DEX on Base (LP operations, swaps, reward collection)
steps: Encode functions for addLiquidity, removeLiquidity, swap, claimRewards
Stable-stable pool pair support (sAMM pools)
Pure functions — no state, no side effects, no chain calls
L2 gas estimation for Base (OP Stack model)
Wired into buildAdapterMap() in index.ts

description: Event reporter — publishes structured TX results to execution:results Redis Stream with schema validation
steps: Builds result payload from TX receipt (status, hash, gas used, block number)
Schema-validates result before publishing to execution:results
Includes correlation ID linking result to original order
Handles both success and failure result types

description: Price feed — multi-source token price ingestion via Alchemy Token Prices API with DefiLlama fallback, staleness detection
steps: Primary source: Alchemy Token Prices API
Fallback source: DefiLlama price API
Supported tokens: USDC, USDT, DAI (stablecoins), AERO
Staleness detection: configurable threshold (default 60s), pauses non-urgent ops when stale
Multi-source validation: reject >2% deviation between sources
Redis cache with configurable TTL

description: Gas monitor — track gas prices on Base for transaction timing decisions, with 24h rolling averages
steps: Base gas price tracking via Blocknative + beaconcha.in
24h rolling average calculation for circuit breaker comparison
L2 gas parameters for Base (OP Stack model)
Redis cache with configurable TTL
Gas data included in MarketSnapshot for strategy evaluation

description: DeFi protocol metrics collector — collect and normalize Aave V3 and Aerodrome protocol metrics (TVL, APY, utilization, pool states)
steps: Aave V3 metrics: supply APY, utilization rate, available liquidity per market
Aerodrome metrics: pool TVL, emission APR, swap volume, AERO price
TVL aggregation across tracked protocols
Graceful degradation: individual provider failure doesn't crash collector
Redis caching of collected metrics with configurable TTL

description: On-chain position reconciliation — query actual on-chain positions via Alchemy and compare against database records, trusting on-chain state
steps: Query on-chain balances and positions via Alchemy Enhanced APIs
Compare against PostgreSQL position records
Trust on-chain state — update database to match on discrepancy
Runs as part of startup recovery sequence
Log discrepancies with structured error details

description: Claude API decision engine — runtime reasoning over strategy reports and portfolio state; assembles structured prompt, calls Claude API, parses schema-compliant execution orders, applies verification gate
steps: System prompt: Claude's role, execution-orders output schema, risk rules (durable across calls)
User message assembled fresh each cycle: objectives → portfolio → risk → reports → market data
Stateless: no conversation memory, fresh prompt each call
Response parsing: validate Claude output against execution-orders schema
Verification gate: orders pass through exposure limits + circuit breaker state check
Hold mode: retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts), enter hold mode if exhausted
Token budget management at API layer

description: Insight synthesis — packages market data, portfolio state, risk context, and strategy reports into structured prompt sections for Claude API
steps: Assembles objectives + strategy guidelines section from STRATEGY.md context
Assembles portfolio state section (positions, allocations, P&L, available capital)
Assembles risk context section (circuit breaker states, exposure levels, recent TX results)
Assembles strategy reports section (observations, signals, recommendations from each strategy)
Assembles market data section (prices, gas, protocol metrics, on-chain events)
Data sections ordered by priority for Claude's attention

description: Strategy protocol and registry — Strategy protocol definition (strategy_id, eval_interval, data_window, evaluate), MarketSnapshot dataclass, StrategyReport structure, auto-discovery from strategies/ directory
steps: Strategy protocol with strategy_id, eval_interval, data_window, evaluate(snapshot) → StrategyReport
MarketSnapshot dataclass: prices, gas, pools, timestamp; engine pre-slices by data_window
StrategyReport: strategy_id, timestamp, observations, signals, recommendation
Signal types: entry_met, exit_met, harvest_ready, rebalance_needed, threshold_approaching
Auto-discovery: any module in strategies/ exporting a Strategy-conforming class is registered
Strategies are analysts — they produce reports, not execution orders

description: Strategy lifecycle manager — manages active/inactive status, per-strategy eval_interval scheduling, and strategy status persistence in PostgreSQL
steps: Active/inactive status per strategy (no intermediate states)
Evaluation scheduling respects each strategy's eval_interval
Status toggle via PostgreSQL or by removing the class file
Strategy status persisted in PostgreSQL, loaded at startup
Concurrent async evaluation of active strategies

description: LEND-001 Aave lending strategy — supplies stablecoins (USDC, USDbC) to Aave V3 on Base, rotates to highest supply APY market when differential exceeds threshold after gas
steps: Implements Strategy protocol with strategy_id='LEND-001'
Entry: target APY exceeds current by ≥0.5% after gas, ≥$1M liquidity, gas amortized in 14 days
Exit: supply APY drops below 1.0%, or TVL circuit breaker triggers
Constraints: max 70% portfolio, min $100 position, net monthly gain after gas >$1
Produces StrategyReport with observations, signals, recommendation

description: LP-001 Aerodrome stable LP strategy — provides liquidity to stable-stable pools (USDC/USDbC, USDC/DAI) on Aerodrome, harvests AERO rewards, swaps to USDC, compounds
steps: Implements Strategy protocol with strategy_id='LP-001'
Entry: emission APR >3.0%, pool TVL >$500K, AERO has sufficient swap liquidity
Exit: APR <1.5%, AERO price drops >50% in 24h, pool TVL <$200K
Constraints: max 30% portfolio, min $100 position, stable-stable only, harvest when AERO >$0.50
Produces StrategyReport with observations, signals, recommendation

description: Portfolio allocator — manages capital allocation across strategies based on per-strategy limits defined in STRATEGY.md
steps: Enforces per-strategy allocation limits (e.g. LEND-001: max 70%, LP-001: max 30%)
Tracks available capital and allocated amounts
Allocation state persisted in PostgreSQL
Provides allocation data for Claude's prompt context

description: Position tracker — real-time view of all open positions with P&L calculation, entry/exit recording, and execution result handling
steps: Open/close positions with P&L calculation
Query positions by status, protocol, asset
Handles execution results from Redis (updates position state on TX success/failure)
State persisted in PostgreSQL, kept in-memory for fast access
Provides position data for Claude's prompt context

description: Rebalancing engine — detects allocation drift and recommends rebalancing when drift exceeds threshold
steps: Compares current allocation against target allocation from STRATEGY.md
Detects drift beyond configurable threshold
Produces rebalance_needed signal for strategy reports
Rebalance decisions go through normal decision pipeline (Claude decides specifics)

description: Drawdown circuit breaker — monitors portfolio value relative to peak; triggers at >20% drawdown with direct emission of unwind orders to Redis, bypassing decision gate
steps: Tracks portfolio peak value continuously
Triggers at >20% drawdown from peak
Direct emission: generates CB:drawdown orders to halt all + unwind to stables
Orders schema-validated before publishing to execution:orders
Runs independently on every market event

description: Per-position loss limit — closes any position that loses >10% of entry value; 24h cooldown tracked via Redis TTL key
steps: Monitors each position's value against entry value
Triggers at >10% loss on any single position
Direct emission: generates CB:position_loss order to close affected position
24h cooldown via Redis TTL key (e.g. cooldown:LEND-001 EX 86400)
Cooldown auto-expires, survives restarts via Redis persistent volume

description: Gas spike circuit breaker — pauses non-urgent operations when gas exceeds 3x 24h average; gate-only, does not emit orders
steps: Compares current gas price against 24h rolling average
Triggers at >3x the 24h average
Gate-only: blocks normal pipeline from executing, does not emit orders
Urgent operations (stop-loss, emergency unwind) bypass gas pause
Clears automatically when gas normalizes

description: TX failure rate monitor — pauses execution and enters hold mode when failure rate exceeds 3 failures per hour; gate-only
steps: Tracks TX failure count in rolling 1-hour window
Triggers at >3 failures/hour
Gate-only: pauses execution, enters hold mode
Clears when failure rate drops below threshold

description: Protocol TVL monitor — monitors TVL of protocols with active positions; triggers withdrawal at >30% TVL drop in 24h with direct emission
steps: Monitors TVL of Aave V3 and Aerodrome continuously
Triggers at >30% TVL drop within 24h window
Direct emission: generates CB:tvl_drop orders to withdraw from affected protocol
Orders schema-validated before publishing
Only monitors protocols where the system has active positions

description: Contract allowlist — application-level contract allowlist enforced by SafeWalletManager.validateOrder() before every TX execution. Interim measure until on-chain Safe guard module (RISK-009) is deployed
steps: Allowlist of permitted contract addresses (case-insensitive matching)
validateOrder() rejects orders targeting non-allowlisted contracts
Runs before every TX execution in the transaction builder
Allowlist configurable via environment variables

description: Oracle manipulation guard — multi-source price validation that rejects operations when price sources deviate by >2%
steps: Compares prices across multiple sources (Alchemy, DefiLlama)
Rejects operations when deviation exceeds 2% between sources
Logs deviation details for audit
Works in conjunction with price feed staleness detection

description: Exposure limit enforcement — enforces portfolio-level concentration limits: per-protocol max, per-asset max, minimum liquid reserve; all configured via env vars
steps: Per-protocol max allocation enforcement
Per-asset max allocation enforcement
Minimum liquid reserve requirement
Limits defined as environment variables, not hardcoded
Checked as part of verification gate before order execution

description: Safe on-chain guard module — deploy AllowlistGuard contract to enforce contract allowlist at the wallet level, unbypassable even if the application is compromised
steps: Deploy AllowlistGuard contract with permitted contract addresses
Attach guard to Safe via setGuard() transaction
Verify on-chain guard matches application-level allowlist (RISK-006)
Guard rejects transactions targeting non-allowlisted contracts at the EVM level

description: State persistence — PostgreSQL for portfolio positions, trade history, strategy statuses, and decision audit log; Redis for market cache, cooldowns (TTL keys), and system status
steps: Portfolio positions persisted in PostgreSQL (holdings, entry prices, timestamps)
Trade history and decision audit log in PostgreSQL
Strategy statuses (active/inactive) in PostgreSQL
Market data cache in Redis (ephemeral, rebuilt on restart)
Circuit breaker cooldowns in Redis TTL keys (auto-expire)
System status (normal/hold) in Redis

description: Startup recovery sequence — load state from PostgreSQL → replay unprocessed Redis Stream messages → on-chain reconciliation → health check → resume or hold
steps: Load portfolio state, positions, strategy statuses from PostgreSQL
Check Redis Streams for unprocessed messages, replay any after last acknowledged
Query on-chain state to verify positions match database records
Reconcile discrepancies (trust on-chain state, update database to match)
Health check connected protocols
Resume normal operation or enter hold mode if reconciliation fails

description: Human-in-the-loop approval gates — high-impact actions require explicit owner approval before execution
steps: High-impact action detection (large trades, protocol withdrawals)
Approval request mechanism
Blocks execution until approval received or timeout
Configurable approval thresholds

description: DEPRECATED: Diagnostic mode — merged into HARNESS-005 (hold mode)
deprecated: Merged into HARNESS-005. Hold mode covers both API failure and irreconcilable state entry paths.

description: Hold mode — system behavior when Claude API is unavailable or irreconcilable state is detected; no new decisions, auto-resume when trigger clears
steps: Tracked as system_status: normal | hold in Redis
Entry path 1: Claude API unavailability (down, timeout after retries, budget exhausted)
Entry path 2: Irreconcilable state discrepancies or critical failures during startup/runtime
No new positions, rebalances, or harvests; existing positions maintained
Strategy evaluation continues (reports stay fresh for Claude's return)
Circuit breakers remain fully active (independent of Claude)
Decision gate stays closed regardless of actionable signals
Structured diagnostic logging for troubleshooting irreconcilable state
Auto-resume when Claude API responds, budget resets, or state is manually reconciled

description: Structured logging — every trade, signal, risk decision, and system event logged as structured JSON with timestamp, service, event, and correlationId
steps: JSON log format: { timestamp, service, event, correlationId, ...data }
Logs to stdout (Railway captures and aggregates)
Trade execution logged with order details and results
Risk decisions logged (circuit breaker triggers, exposure limit rejections)
System events logged (startup, shutdown, hold mode transitions)

description: Performance dashboard — real-time tracking of portfolio value, cumulative P&L, Sharpe ratio, and position breakdown
steps: Portfolio value tracking over time
Cumulative P&L calculation
Sharpe ratio computation
Position breakdown by strategy and protocol
Dashboard data assembled from PostgreSQL + real-time market data

description: P&L attribution — break down portfolio performance by strategy, protocol, asset, and time period
steps: P&L breakdown by strategy (LEND-001, LP-001)
P&L breakdown by protocol (Aave V3, Aerodrome)
P&L breakdown by asset (USDC, USDbC, DAI)
Time-period filtering (daily, weekly, monthly, custom range)
Data sourced from trade history in PostgreSQL

description: Integration test suite — E2E lifecycle tests, circuit breaker integration tests, schema validation tests, and startup recovery tests
steps: E2E lifecycle test: market event → strategy eval → decision gate → order → execution → result
Circuit breaker integration: drawdown trigger → emergency unwind → position closed
Schema validation: all message types validated against shared schemas in both services
Startup recovery: state load → stream replay → reconciliation
All tests pass without external dependencies (mocked providers)

description: Calldata validation tests — encode/decode roundtrip tests for all protocol adapters, verifying ABI correctness and selector uniqueness
steps: Aave V3: supply/withdraw encode roundtrip with address and selector verification
Aerodrome: addLiquidity/removeLiquidity/swap encode roundtrip
Function selector uniqueness across all adapters
Gas estimation sanity checks for Base L2

description: Sepolia live tests — on-chain contract interaction tests against Sepolia testnet, skipped by default, enabled via ALCHEMY_SEPOLIA_HTTP_URL env var
steps: Contract existence verification on Sepolia
Aave V3 getReserveData call succeeds
Encoded calldata accepted by contract (estimateGas succeeds)
Tests skip when ALCHEMY_SEPOLIA_HTTP_URL not set
Tests isolated from unit test suite

description: Railway production deployment — Dockerfiles for both services, Railway config, persistent volumes for Redis + PostgreSQL, git-push deploy trigger
steps: ts-executor Dockerfile: multi-stage build (Node 22 Alpine, dev/prod targets)
py-engine Dockerfile: multi-stage build (Python 3.12-slim, uv-based, dev/prod targets)
Railway persistent volumes for PostgreSQL and Redis
Deploy trigger: git push to main → container rebuild
Startup sequence runs on deploy (Section 7 of system design)
Rollback via Railway built-in mechanism
